title: User registered
description: auth-microservice's first event. DELIBERATELY GENERIC and ecosystem-owned, not growth-owned: it must be reusable by S6, S10 and MS-P. No gsid, no experimentId, no workspaceId, no growth concept may be added here - see EP-005 W3 and D-005 s3. workspaceId is deliberately absent: it is a growth concept, and growth-core resolves it on consumption (C-005 s1, owner 2026-07-21). correlationId is opaque to auth: round-tripped via `state`, never interpreted, never persisted beyond this event. Emitted on PROVEN identity only - password registration, OAuth callback, and magic-link VERIFICATION - never on magic-link request or contact provisioning (C-005 s2.2b).

correlationId.description: Envelope-level tracing id, always present. Distinct from payload.correlationId, which is the growth join key and is absent on direct signups.

userId.description: Identity of the registration. Replaces the never-minted registrationId.

correlationId.description: Opaque, from the `state` parameter. Absent on direct signups that did not come through a growth landing - a normal case, not an error.

applicationContext.description: Which application the registration came from. Generic - not an experiment id.

registrationMethod.description: Which proven-identity path produced this registration. Contact provisioning is not one of them - it emits no event.

email.description: OPTIONAL - a user registered by phone may have none.
